summary: WEB REGRESSION guard PASSED for src/font.ts edit (native iOS fontWeight-stripping). On web (390x844) login via OTP (phone=9059721442, code=123456) lands on Home; MU hero banner image (08bgdgj4_image.png) renders; 'Explore campus' heading computes font-family 'Sora, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif' — Sora correctly applied. All 9 sampled icon nodes (Ionicons/MaterialCommunityIcons in bottom tabs, service tile, chevrons, advisory chip) render with PUA glyphs (codepoints 0xE000+: 62327, 61747, 62338, 62339, 983099, 62581, 62591, 62633, 62634) — NO tofu boxes. Services tab shows 3-column grid; tapping service-tile-mess opens /modules/mess which renders all 4 meal pills (breakfast/lunch/snacks/dinner) + mess-menu-card + 3 mess crowd cards. Alerts tab renders 3 accordion announcements. Profile tab renders avatar + 3 profile rows + 4 settings rows + Sign Out button. Mark Attendance via Home hero-cta (Staff role 'Check in now') renders action-in, action-out, 4 attendance-type chips and 3 tabs [today/history/stats]. No console errors. NO 'indexed property on CSSStyleDeclaration' messages. Only carry-over RN-Web deprecation warnings ('shadow*' and 'props.pointerEvents') — pre-existing, already documented in iteration_4. The web path was untouched by the font.ts edit (only the native Text.render branch changed); confirmed unchanged behaviour on web.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings still present: "shadow*" style props are deprecated, use "boxShadow"; props.pointerEvents is deprecated, use style.pointerEvents. Reference: /app/frontend/src/theme.ts clay.* recipes. Not introduced by this iteration.

action_items: [INFO] No fixes required. src/font.ts native patch update is web-safe — web path uses pure CSS @font-face and was not modified. The iOS fontWeight-strip fix can only be validated on a physical iOS device / native build (Expo Go), per task note.
[LOW / carryover] Replace shadow* props with boxShadow and move pointerEvents into style in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts to silence RN-Web deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: src/font.ts: web branch is unchanged (early-return Platform.OS==='web' → applyWebStylesheet()), so the iOS-specific fontWeight strip cannot impact web. Verified: 'Explore campus' computes 'Sora, system-ui, …' on web.

Icon protection logic is still correct: native patch returns early when flat.fontFamily is set (icon glyph fonts), and the web stylesheet uses :not([style*="font-family"]) to exclude inline icon families. Result: all icons render as real glyphs.

On native, dropping fontWeight when injecting a Sora variant is the right call — each Sora weight (Sora-Bold, Sora-ExtraBold, …) is a separate iOS family; mixing fontWeight with a named family makes iOS attempt to bolden further and silently fall back to system. Cannot validate on web.
Style normalisation `{ ...rest, fontFamily }` produces a single object, avoiding the RN-Web nested-array style edge case mentioned in the comment — good defensive practice.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 8 web regression checks: login, home hero, Sora font, icons-no-tofu, services/mess, alerts, profile, attendance)

seed_data_creation: None (mock-mode app; auth fully mocked in /app/frontend/src/mock.ts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is MOCK MODE (no backend calls). OTP login: any phone + code 123456. 9059721442 → Staff (Visvesh, Warden); Home hero CTA for staff = 'Check in now' → /modules/attendance. Bottom tab labels (Home/Services/Alerts/Profile) collide with Home category-pill labels ('Services'); use page.get_by_text('Services').last to target the bottom nav. Mess testIDs: meal-{breakfast,lunch,snacks,dinner}, mess-menu-card, mess-card-{id}, mess-back. Attendance testIDs: action-in, action-out, atttype-{Office|Work From Home|Client Visit|Field Ops}, tab-{today,history,stats}. Carry-over warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents) are noise — ignore unless asked to address.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no regression. src/font.ts web branch unchanged; the iOS-only fontWeight-strip is on the native render-patch path and cannot affect the web preview.
